pull.title: doc: Fix fee field in getblock RPC result
pull.body: The `fee` field in the `getblock` RPC result (verbosity 2 and 3) may be omitted when block undo data is not available. Marking it optional in the `RPCResult` aligns the documented schema with the runtime behavior.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [instagibbs](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34702#pullrequestreview-3876553031), [w0xlt](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34702#pullrequestreview-3878124056), [mercie-ux](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34702#issuecomment-3986477475), [luke-jr](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34702#pullrequestreview-3909308288), [satsfy](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34702#issuecomment-4018073721) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.
I can't recall, but would a functional test fail in master when it doesn't end up existing in a response? If so, worth making a regression test.

tree 045bfcd3aa63f0ae6fc39604f21cd43e2d39ab2b
parent 9cad97f6cdf1bd660732cd10e844a6a7e0771ea0
author nervana21 <205626986+nervana21@users.noreply.github.com> 1772303780 -0500
committer nervana21 <205626986+nervana21@users.noreply.github.com> 1772382573 -0500

doc: Fix `fee` field in `getblock` RPC result

The `fee` field in the `getblock` RPC result (verbosity 2 and 3) may be
omitted when block undo data is not available. Marking it optional in
the `RPCResult` aligns the documented schema with the runtime behavior.

events.message:
doc: Fix `fee` field in `getblock` RPC result

The `fee` field in the `getblock` RPC result (verbosity 2 and 3) may be
omitted when block undo data is not available. Marking it optional in
the `RPCResult` aligns the documented schema with the runtime behavior.

events.body:
> I can't recall, but would a functional test fail in master when it doesn't end up existing in a response? If so, worth making a regression test.

This behavior is already covered by a functional test: 

<https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/744d47fcee0d32a71154292699bfdecf954a6065/test/functional/rpc_blockchain.py#L766-L767>

So I think only the documentation needs to be updated. Is there anything else that should be considered?

ACK https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/f580cc7e9f26331f7f03a8bbc5722521eb159bb2

I wonder why it was not there. If at some point in time it was not optional.
